page: Amazon Web Services a1.4xlarge is an ARM-based virtual machine powered by the first-generation AWS Graviton processor. It features 16 dedicated physical cores based on the ARMv8 Cortex-A72 architecture, running at a clock speed of 2.3 GHz. The instance is equipped with 32.0 GB of system memory, providing 2.0 GB of RAM per core, and operates on the AWS Nitro hypervisor. It does not include local storage or GPU accelerators, and it provides a baseline network bandwidth of 5 Gbps.

Benchmark data indicates average performance in multi-core processing and cached memory bandwidth, with stress-ng multi-core scoring 23858.27 and cached small-block read bandwidth reaching 566.18 GB/sec. However, single-core performance is weak, as evidenced by a Geekbench single-core score of 409.0 and a stress-ng single-core score of 1491.48. Uncached memory operations show limitations, with large-block read and write bandwidths categorized as weak and poor, respectively. The server demonstrates average capabilities in specific workloads like software compilation with Clang and Redis SET operations, but struggles with single-threaded compression and text processing.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the a1.4xlarge offers a budget-friendly entry point into ARM-based cloud computing, leveraging first-generation Graviton hardware to deliver dedicated physical cores at a lower resource density. Due to its balanced multi-core performance and average database operation metrics, this instance is suited for scale-out workloads, containerized microservices, web serving, and development environments optimized for the arm64 architecture. It is less suitable for memory-intensive databases, high-performance computing, or single-threaded applications.
description: Amazon Web Services a1.4xlarge is an ARM-based instance powered by the first-generation AWS Graviton processor. It features 16 dedicated physical cores on the ARMv8 Cortex-A72 architecture running at 2.3 GHz, paired with 32.0 GB of system memory and a 5 Gbps baseline network interface. Operating on the Nitro hypervisor, this server does not include local storage or GPU accelerators. Performance benchmarks show average multi-core capabilities, such as a stress-ng multi-core score of 23858.27 and 8.36 million Redis SET operations per second, contrasted by weak single-core and uncached memory bandwidth performance. Qualitatively, it represents a cost-efficient option for arm64 development, containerized microservices, and scale-out web serving workloads that benefit from dedicated physical cores without requiring high single-threaded performance.
og_description: Amazon Web Services a1.4xlarge is an ARM-based server with 16 dedicated Graviton cores, 32 GB RAM, and a 5 Gbps network baseline, optimized for cost-efficient scale-out arm64 workloads.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services a1.4xlarge is an ARM-based instance featuring 16 dedicated Graviton cores and 32 GB RAM for cost-effective scale-out workloads.
tagline: An ARM-based virtual server featuring dedicated physical cores and balanced multi-core performance for cost-efficient scale-out workloads.
bullet_points: Powered by 16 dedicated ARMv8 Cortex-A72 physical cores at 2.3 GHz
Equipped with 32.0 GB of system memory and 5 Gbps baseline network bandwidth
Delivers average multi-core performance and 8.36 million Redis SET operations per second
Shows weak single-core and uncached memory write bandwidth performance
Best suited for arm64 software development, containerized microservices, and web serving
